ucs.title: [LLM] Volt Typhoon LOTL discovery burst — chained native recon binaries on one host
ucs.description: Hunts the signature 'hands-on-keyboard' Living-Off-The-Land discovery sequence that CISA AA24-038A and Microsoft attribute to Volt Typhoon (Vanguard Panda / VOLTZITE): rapid-fire chains of wmic / net / nltest / netstat / tasklist / systeminfo / ipconfig / whoami / arp / quser issued from a single host inside a short window, often via cmd.exe spawned from a wsmprovhost / svchost parent after edge-device pivot. Volt Typhoon almost never drops malware on the initial host — this discovery burst IS the beachhead.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Processes.process) as cmds dc(Processes.process_name) as unique_recon_bins from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("wmic.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","netstat.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","whoami.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","reg.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","netsh.exe") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where unique_recon_bins >= 5 | sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
// Volt Typhoon (G1017) — LOTL discovery burst
let window = 10m;
let recon_bins = dynamic(["wmic.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","netstat.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","whoami.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","reg.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","netsh.exe"]);
let recon_keywords = dynamic(["systeminfo","ipconfig /all","netstat -ano","tasklist","net user","net group","net localgroup","net accounts","net share","net use","nltest /domain_trusts","nltest /dclist","whoami /all","whoami /groups","whoami /priv","arp -a","route print","query user","quser","wmic computersystem","wmic os","wmic process","wmic ntdomain","wmic useraccount","hostname"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where FileName in~ (recon_bins)
     or ProcessCommandLine has_any (recon_keywords)
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| summarize
    UniqueReconBins   = dcount(FileName),
    ReconHits         = count(),
    FirstSeen         = min(Timestamp),
    LastSeen          = max(Timestamp),
    SampleCmds        = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 30),
    Parents           = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
    GrandParents      = make_set(InitiatingProcessParentFileName, 10)
    by DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, window)
| where UniqueReconBins >= 5         // 5+ distinct recon binaries inside one 10-min bucket
| where ReconHits      >= 8          // and at least 8 invocations total
| extend WsmprovhostParent = iff(Parents has "wsmprovhost.exe" or GrandParents has "wsmprovhost.exe", true, false)   // WinRM-spawned = high signal
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Volt Typhoon (G1017) — LOTL discovery burst (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let window = 10m;
let recon_bins = dynamic(["wmic.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","netstat.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","whoami.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","reg.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","netsh.exe"]);
let recon_keywords = dynamic(["systeminfo","ipconfig /all","netstat -ano","net user","net group","net localgroup","net accounts","nltest /domain_trusts","nltest /dclist","whoami /all","whoami /groups","whoami /priv","arp -a","route print","query user","quser","wmic computersystem","wmic ntdomain","wmic useraccount"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])), CmdLower = tolower(CommandLine)
| where Bin in (recon_bins) or CmdLower has_any (recon_keywords)
| summarize
    UniqueReconBins = dcount(Bin),
    ReconHits       = count(),
    FirstSeen       = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen        = max(TimeGenerated),
    SampleCmds      = make_set(CommandLine, 30),
    Parents         = make_set(ParentProcessName, 10)
    by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, window)
| where UniqueReconBins >= 5
| where ReconHits      >= 8
| extend WsmprovhostParent = iff(tostring(Parents) has "wsmprovhost.exe", true, false)
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Volt Typhoon's CISA-published playbook (AA24-038A) and Microsoft's MSRC profile both highlight that the actor 'almost exclusively relies on living-off-the-land techniques' — they do not drop malware on the initial victim. This detection mirrors the actual command sequences pulled from CISA forensics: wmic ntdomain, nltest /domain_trusts, net user, netstat -ano, systeminfo, etc., bursting from one

ucs.title: [LLM] Volt Typhoon NTDS.dit theft via ntdsutil IFM snapshot or vssadmin shadow copy + esentutl
ucs.description: Hunts the precise credential-access tradecraft Volt Typhoon (Bronze Silhouette / VOLTZITE) uses on compromised domain controllers: invoking ntdsutil with the 'ifm' / 'create full' / 'activate instance ntds' subcommands to write an offline copy of NTDS.dit + SYSTEM hive, OR creating a Volume Shadow Copy and using esentutl/copy to lift ntds.dit out of it. CISA AA24-038A explicitly cites this as a Volt Typhoon credential-dumping pattern.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ( (Processes.process_name="ntdsutil.exe" AND (Processes.process="*ifm*" OR Processes.process="*create full*" OR Processes.process="*create copy*" OR Processes.process="*activate instance ntds*")) OR (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*create*shadow*") OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy*create*") OR (Processes.process_name="esentutl.exe" AND (Processes.process="*ntds.dit*" OR Processes.process="*/y*" OR Processes.process="*/vss*")) OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND Processes.process="*ntdsutil*") OR (Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" AND Processes.process="*ntds.dit*") ) by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
// Volt Typhoon (G1017) — NTDS.dit dump via ntdsutil IFM or vssadmin+esentutl
let ntdsutil_signal =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where (FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe")
         or (FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "ntdsutil")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ifm", "create full", "create copy", "activate instance ntds")
    | extend Technique = "ntdsutil-IFM";
let vss_signal =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("create","shadow"))
         or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","create"))
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "win32_shadowcopy")
    | extend Technique = "vss-create";
let esentutl_signal =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName =~ "esentutl.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ntds.dit","/y","/vss","hivelist","system32\\config\\system")
    | extend Technique = "esentutl-copy";
let copy_signal =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileCopied")
    | where FileName =~ "ntds.dit" or FileName =~ "system" or FileName =~ "security"
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\NTDS"
         and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\config"
    | extend Technique = "ntds-file-staged", DeviceName = DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
             ProcessCommandLine = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName = FileName,
             InitiatingProcessFileName = InitiatingProcessFileName;
union isfuzzy=true ntdsutil_signal, vss_signal, esentutl_signal, copy_signal
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| project Timestamp, Technique, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Volt Typhoon (G1017) — NTDS.dit theft (Sentinel)
let ntdsutil_signal =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where (NewProcessName endswith "\\ntdsutil.exe")
         or (NewProcessName endswith_cs "cmd.exe" and CommandLine has "ntdsutil")
         or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has "ntdsutil")
    | where CommandLine has_any ("ifm","create full","create copy","activate instance ntds")
    | extend Technique = "ntdsutil-IFM";
let vss_signal =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where (NewProcessName endswith "\\vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("create","shadow"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\wmic.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","create"))
         or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has "win32_shadowcopy")
    | extend Technique = "vss-create";
let esentutl_signal =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\esentutl.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("ntds.dit","/y","/vss","hivelist")
    | extend Technique = "esentutl-copy";
union ntdsutil_signal, vss_signal, esentutl_signal
| where Account !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Technique, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Volt Typhoon NTDS.dit Extraction via ntdsutil IFM Snapshot
id: 7e1b3a9f-2c5d-4d6a-9b3e-volttyphoon-ntds
status: experimental
description: Detects ntdsutil invoked with IFM/create-full/activate-instance subcommands used by Volt Typhoon (G1017) to dump the Active Directory database offline on a compromised domain controller.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-038a
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1017/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2024/02/07
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.003
  - attack.g1017
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    - Image|endswith: '\ntdsutil.exe'
    - OriginalFileName: 'ntdsutil.exe'
  selection_args:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' ifm'
      - 'create full'
      - 'create copy'
      - 'activate instance ntds'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_image and selection_args and not filter_machine_account
falsepositives:
  - Documented IT operations performing legitimate AD backups via NTDSUTIL IFM (rare; allowlist by host + service account)
level: high
ucs.rationale: CISA AA24-038A specifically calls out Volt Typhoon dumping NTDS.dit as a primary credential-access objective and cites both the ntdsutil 'create full' / IFM workflow and shadow-copy + esentutl extraction as observed methods. Both routes have near-zero legitimate use outside of formally scheduled AD backups — the IFM subcommand in particular is almost never run interactively. Combining the ntdsutil
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
